title: SOCKS5 proxy support for Tor / I2P
description:
Add a --proxy flag so nie clients route their relay WebSocket connection through a SOCKS5 proxy (Tor, I2P, or any SOCKS5 proxy). The relay sees only the exit node IP, not the client's real IP. Highest-value privacy improvement with minimal code change.

## Scope

### CLI flag
  --proxy <url>    SOCKS5 proxy URL, e.g. socks5://127.0.0.1:9050 for Tor
                   or socks5h://127.0.0.1:9050 (socks5h = proxy resolves DNS, recommended for Tor)
  Also readable from config.toml: proxy = 'socks5h://127.0.0.1:9050'

### Implementation
- tokio-tungstenite supports SOCKS5 via the tokio-socks crate (pure async, no C deps)
- In transport.rs: if proxy is set, create a Socks5Stream wrapping the TcpStream before the WebSocket upgrade
- TLS still applies on top: proxy tunnels the TCP connection, TLS encrypts the payload
- For .onion addresses (socks5h://): DNS resolution happens inside Tor; the client never resolves the hostname directly

### Lightwalletd proxy
- Optionally: also proxy the lightwalletd gRPC connection through SOCKS5
- tonic supports custom connectors; a Socks5 connector wrapping the gRPC channel hides Zcash wallet sync from the ISP
- This is a separate flag: --lwd-proxy (lightwalletd may need a different proxy than the relay)

### Warn on clearnet .onion
- If relay URL is a .onion address and no proxy is set: warn!() that the .onion address will not resolve without a SOCKS5 proxy
- Do not hard-error (user may have a split DNS setup)

## Security notes
- socks5h (proxy-side DNS resolution) is required for Tor; socks5 (client-side DNS) leaks the hostname to local DNS
- Log a warning if socks5:// is used with a .onion relay URL (should be socks5h://)
- Never log the proxy URL at INFO level (it may contain auth credentials in future)

## Dependencies
- nie-hlns (JSON-RPC migration) — transport layer is being reworked there; proxy support lands cleanly on top
